data: No description is provided, but a statue base seems likely from the fragmentary text recorded.

The main text appears to consist of an honorific followed by at least four lines of what Kaible conjectures to be a verse couplet; a further three lines of letters are described as sitting to the right of the main text, in an uncertain relationship.
Kaibel's text based upon the manuscript of Tardia records lunate epsilon and sigma, with cursive omega.
Third, or more likely fourth century CE based upon the text.
Recorded as 'in casa del S.D. Vincenzo Clavica", in a manuscript of Francesco Tardia (1732-1778).
Inscriptiones Graecae Siciliae et Italiae, additis graecis Galliae Hispaniae, Britanniae, Germaniae inscriptionibus

G. Kaibel, Inscriptiones Graecae Siciliae et Italiae, additis graecis Galliae Hispaniae, Britanniae, Germaniae inscriptionibus, Inscriptiones Graecae consilio et auctoritate Academiae Litterarum Regiae Borussicae Editae. Volumen XIV., XIV (Berlin: Georgius Reimerus, 1890)
Inscriptiones Graecae ad res Romanas pertinentes
R. Cagnat, J. Toutain, and P. Jouguet, Inscriptiones Graecae ad res Romanas pertinentes, 4 vols (Paris: Ernest Leroux, 1906)
La Sicilia da Sesto Pompeo a Diocleziano
Giacomo Manganaro, «La Sicilia da Sesto Pompeo a Diocleziano», Aufstieg und Niedergang der romischen Welt 2.11.1 (1988): 3–89
Manganaro, 1988, La Sicilia da Sesto Pompeo a Diocleziano

Apparatus criticus

  • Text based upon the edition of Kaibel, who makes a number of assumptions about mistranscribed letters in Tardia

translation

commentary

Potentially a public honorific, including a verse epigram. It is unclear what relationship the short (incomplete? fragmentary?) text inscribed alongside the main text has to the main text.
